description: Complete documentation site for the Orbiter Python multi-agent framework. 153 markdown files served via Astro 5 with warm parchment design system, animated orbital illustrations, Pagefind search, and zero framework JS.

userStories.title: Install dependencies and configure Astro with Tailwind v4
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the project bootstrapped with all required dependencies so that Astro, Tailwind CSS v4, and TypeScript are properly configured.
acceptanceCriteria: package.json includes: astro@^5.17, @tailwindcss/vite@^4, tailwindcss@^4, @astrojs/sitemap@^3, @fontsource-variable/bricolage-grotesque@^5, clsx@^2, tailwind-merge@^2
package.json devDependencies include: typescript@^5, pagefind@^1
astro.config.mjs configures Tailwind v4 via vite.plugins: [tailwindcss()] (NOT the older @astrojs/tailwind)
astro.config.mjs includes @astrojs/sitemap integration
tsconfig.json extends astro/tsconfigs/strict
npm run dev starts the dev server without errors
npm run build produces output in dist/
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Create global CSS with design system color tokens and font faces
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the design system's color tokens, font faces, and Tailwind theme mapping defined so all components share a consistent visual language.
acceptanceCriteria: src/styles/global.css exists with @import 'tailwindcss' and @import '@fontsource-variable/bricolage-grotesque'
CSS custom properties defined: --zen-paper (#f2f0e3 light / #1f1f1f dark), --zen-dark (#2e2e2e light / #d1cfc0 dark), --zen-muted, --zen-subtle
[data-theme='dark'] overrides all custom properties to dark mode values
@theme {} block maps tokens to Tailwind: paper, dark, coral (#F76F53), zen-blue (#6287f5), zen-green (#63f78b), muted, subtle
@font-face declarations for Junicode Roman and Italic (woff2 files in public/fonts/)
Body defaults: font-family Bricolage Grotesque, font-weight 500, bg-paper, text-dark
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Junicode woff2 files must be placed in public/fonts/ manually or downloaded. Use placeholder files if not available.

userStories.title: Add animation keyframes and prose styles to global CSS
userStories.description: As a developer, I need animation keyframes, stagger delays, reduced-motion support, and markdown prose styles so scroll animations and rendered content look correct.
acceptanceCriteria: @keyframes defined in global.css: zenReveal (blur+translateY+opacity), zenFade (blur+opacity), zenScale (blur+scale+opacity), heroEntrance
Stagger delay CSS: [data-delay='1'] through [data-delay='6'] with 0.15s increments
@media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) resets all animations to final state

Prose styles scoped under .prose class: paragraphs (text-dark/80 leading-relaxed), inline code (bg-dark/[0.06] rounded-md), links (text-coral with underline), bold, blockquotes (border-l-2 border-coral/40), tables, lists
section-divider class adds ::before pseudo-element gradient line (transparent to --zen-dark at 8% opacity to transparent)
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Create cn() merge utility
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a class-name merge utility so components can accept class props without Tailwind conflicts.
acceptanceCriteria: src/utils/merge.ts exports a cn() function that wraps clsx + tailwind-merge
cn('px-4', 'px-6') returns 'px-6' (tailwind-merge resolves conflicts)
cn('text-red-500', undefined, false, 'font-bold') returns 'text-red-500 font-bold' (clsx filters falsy)
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Create BaseLayout with theme detection and animation observer
userStories.description: As a user, I need a consistent HTML shell that handles theme detection before first paint and triggers scroll-based animations.
acceptanceCriteria: src/layouts/BaseLayout.astro renders <!DOCTYPE html>, <html lang='en' data-theme='light'>, <head>, <body>
<head> includes: global.css import, font preloads for Bricolage Grotesque, meta viewport
Blocking inline <script> in <head> reads localStorage theme, falls back to prefers-color-scheme, defaults to light, sets data-theme on <html>
Body contains <script> that creates IntersectionObserver for [data-animate] elements (threshold 0.05, rootMargin -40px bottom), adds is-visible class, unobserves
Skip-to-content link: Skip to content
Props interface accepts title: string and description?: string
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Configure content collection with glob loader for docs-source
userStories.description: As a developer, I need Astro Content Collections configured to read markdown files from docs-source/ so docs stay in sync without file copying.
acceptanceCriteria: src/content.config.ts defines a docs collection using glob({ pattern: '**/*.md', base: './docs-source' })
Schema uses z.object() with all optional fields: title (string), description (string), order (number), category (string), draft (boolean default false)
getCollection('docs') returns entries for all markdown files in docs-source/
Entry IDs match file paths: docs-source/guides/agents.md produces an entry referencing that file
Files without frontmatter parse successfully (all schema fields optional)
Dev server hot-reloads when a file in docs-source/ is modified
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Create remark plugin for title extraction from first heading
userStories.description: As a developer, I need titles extracted from the first # heading so pages without frontmatter titles display correctly.
acceptanceCriteria: src/plugins/remark-extract-title.ts finds the first # heading node in the markdown AST
Extracted title injected into file.data.astro.frontmatter.title
The # heading node is removed from the AST (layout renders title separately)
Files with a frontmatter title field are NOT overridden
Files without any # heading still build without error (title falls back to slug-based name)
Plugin registered in astro.config.mjs under markdown.remarkPlugins

index.md references stripped to directory path
External URLs (https://...) left unchanged
Anchor-only links (#section) left unchanged
Plugin registered in astro.config.mjs under markdown.remarkPlugins
